subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Akaye - Kika ati Oye Akaye
context: Reading comprehension passages on contemporary social issues like drug abuse, religious conflicts, road safety, women and children's rights, with focus on understanding content and answering questions

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Akaye - Akanlọ Ede ati Koko Oro
context: Identifying and explaining idiomatic expressions, literary devices, and main themes within comprehension passages to deepen understanding of Yoruba language usage

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Onka - Egbaa si Egbaawaa (2,000 - 20,000)
context: Learning to read and write numbers in Yoruba from 2,000 to 20,000, understanding the pattern of Egbaa (2,000), Egbaaji (4,000), Egbaata (6,000), and converting between figures and words

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Onka - Kika Onka Aarin (Numbers In Between)
context: Reading and writing numbers between thousands in Yoruba, such as 4,020 (okoo le legbaaji) and 5,080 (orin le leedegbaata), mastering intermediate number expressions

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Akoto Yoruba - Ipinnu JCC 1974
context: Understanding the 1974 Joint Consultative Committee (JCC) decision on Yoruba orthography including vowels (faweli), consonants (konsonanti), tone marks (ami ohun), and syllabication (ipinro)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ise Oruko ati Aropo Oruko - Oluwa
context: Understanding the function of nouns and pronouns as subject (Oluwa) in sentences, identifying how they initiate actions and form the basis of Yoruba sentence structure

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ise Oruko ati Aropo Oruko - Abo
context: Learning about nouns and pronouns functioning as objects in sentences, including direct object (Abo fun oro ise) and indirect object (Abo fun oro atokun)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ise Oruko ati Aropo Oruko - Eyan
context: Understanding how nouns and pronouns function as complements (Eyan) in sentences, replacing other nouns to complete meaning and maintain grammatical structure

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Gbolohun - Identifying Nouns and Pronouns
context: Practical exercises in identifying and underlining nouns and pronouns serving different grammatical functions (subject, object, complement) within various sentence structures

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Ibudo ati Ahunpo
context: Studying selected Yoruba historical plays, analyzing plot structure (ibudo), sequence of events (ahunpo), and how the story unfolds from beginning to end

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Asa to Suyo ati Koko Oro
context: Analyzing cultural values (asa to suyo) embedded in historical plays and identifying major themes (koko oro) that relate to contemporary society

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Eda Itan ati Ifiwaweda
context: Studying characters (eda itan) in historical plays, analyzing their characterization (ifiwaweda), roles, and relationships within the dramatic narrative

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Ilo Ede ati Isere
context: Understanding language use (ilo ede) in plays, learning moral lessons (eko), and participating in dramatic performances (isere) to bring characters to life

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Asayan Ewi - Kika ati Oye
context: Reading and understanding selected Yoruba poems on contemporary themes like gender equality, women's issues, economic systems, religious/cultural tolerance, and youth lifestyle challenges

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Asayan Ewi - Ona-ede ati Isowolo Ede
context: Identifying and analyzing poetic devices (ona-ede) and figurative language (isowolo ede) used in Yoruba poetry to enhance meaning and artistic expression

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Asayan Ewi - Koko Oro ati Eko
context: Extracting themes (koko oro) and moral lessons (eko) from poems, relating poetic messages to real-life situations and contemporary societal issues

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Ibudo, Ahunpo ati Asa
context: Studying selected Yoruba prose fiction, analyzing setting (ibudo), plot development (ahunpo), and cultural practices (asa) reflected in the narrative

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Eda Itan ati Ifiwaweda
context: Analyzing characters (eda itan) in prose fiction, their characterization (ifiwaweda), personality traits, and roles in advancing the story's themes

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Koko Oro ati Ona-ede
context: Identifying main themes (koko oro) in prose narratives and analyzing stylistic devices (ona-ede) and language techniques (isowolo ede) used by the author

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Afiwe Isele ati Oju Aye
context: Comparing events in fictional narratives with real-life situations, understanding how literature reflects and comments on contemporary societal issues

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Iwa Ika si Omolakeji - Latijo
context: Understanding traditional forms of cruelty and wickedness against fellow humans in ancient times including slavery (ikonileru), human sacrifice (fifi eniyan rubo), and other injustices

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Iwa Ika si Omolakeji - Lode Oni
context: Examining contemporary forms of cruelty including kidnapping (jijinigbe), murder (ipaniyan), child labor (lilo omo nilokulo), fraud (ifomosowo), and domestic violence (lilu aya)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Didekun Iwa Ika - Ona Abojuto
context: Learning ways to prevent and stop wickedness in society through peace promotion (gbigba alaafia laaye), empathy (fifi ara-eni si ipo omolakeji), and appropriate legal punishment (agbofinro)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Esin Ibile - Ohun Mimo
context: Understanding Yoruba traditional religious beliefs about sacred objects (ohun mimo) such as white cloth (aso funfun), white chalk (efun), white calabash (igba funfun), snails (igbin), and sacred water (omi ajipon)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Esin Ibile - Eewo Orisa ati Iwa Mimo
context: Learning about taboos (eewo) associated with deities (orisa) and moral values like truthfulness (otito) and justice (ododo) in Yoruba traditional religion, comparing with other religions

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Owo Sise - Oriki ati Pataki
context: Understanding the meaning and importance of work (owo sise) in Yoruba society, learning praise poetry for various occupations (oriki owo sise) and why work is valued

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Owo Sise - Orisirisi Owo ati Ipolowo
context: Identifying various traditional and modern occupations among the Yoruba people and understanding marketing strategies (ipolowo oja) used in Yoruba business culture

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Owo Sise - Dinku Airisese Odo
context: Understanding how engaging in productive work can reduce youth unemployment (airisese odo) and the positive impact of entrepreneurship on society

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itoju Alaboyun - Igbagbo ati Asiko
context: Understanding Yoruba beliefs about fertility, pregnancy (oyun), child-bearing including concepts like agan (barrenness), abiku (spirit children), blood genotype compatibility, and appropriate age for pregnancy

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Aroko - Ibanisorọ Atijo ati Ode Oni
context: Learning about traditional Yoruba messaging system (aroko) as ancient communication method, types of aroko (aale, aga, itufu), symbolic objects used (owo eyo, ikarahun igbin, iye) and modern equivalents
